title: Cotula longipes
intro: Cotula longipes, also known as the Cotula daisy, is a small, annual or biennial herb belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reducing fever. Bioactive constituents of Cotula longipes include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cotula longipes is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to create remedies for various ailments.
taxonomy: Cotula longipes is classified as a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It belongs to the genus Cotula, which comprises several species of annual and perennial plants. The genus Cotula is further classified under the subfamily Asteroideae, which includes many species of daisies and related plants. Cotula longipes is native to New Zealand and is a rare and endangered species. Its classification is also supported by molecular studies that have confirmed its relationship to other members of the Asteraceae family.
